title: The Bleeding Seam
intro: A hairline seam of the Fracture has opened in the hillside, and it bleeds raw magic into the air like heat off a stone. There is mana here for the taking — but the Fracture is a wound, and working it invites it to open wider.

advisors.text: Behind heavy wards, we can drink from it and give little back, Archon. Careless, and we trade a full reserve for a torn hillside.
Or we seal it and lose the mana. A wound fed is a wound that grows — I would not feed this one.

choices.label: Tap the seam behind heavy wards

win.text: The wards hold, the seam gives up a rich draught of mana, and the Fracture barely widens for it. Cleanly done.
win.saga: The coven tapped a bleeding Fracture-seam behind heavy wards, drawing rich mana while the Fracture barely stirred.

lose.text: You get the mana — the wards do not hold. The seam yawns wider as you draw, and the hillside groans with the Fracture's growing.
lose.saga: The coven drew mana from a Fracture-seam, but the wards failed and the wound gaped wider for it.

win.text: You close the seam with patient wardwork. A little bled-off mana settles in your reserves, and the hillside stills.
win.saga: You sealed a bleeding Fracture-seam rather than work it, gathering only what had bled off and easing the wound.
